clean_story: "Boredom isn’t emptiness—it’s a rebellion. Your brain isn’t starving for stimulation; it’s rejecting the garbage you’ve trained it to crave. Studies show that after just 6 minutes of unstructured time, 67% of men and 25% of women choose electric shocks over silence (Wilson et al., 2014)—not because boredom hurts, but because avoiding it has rewired you into a dopamine addict. The paradox? The more you flee boredom with scrolls, snacks, or ‘productivity,’ the more your capacity for deep thought atrophies. Victorian-era diaries reveal people endured weeks of monotony without existential meltdowns—because they hadn’t outsourced their attention spans to algorithms. Here’s the kicker: Your boredom isn’t a void. It’s the first honest signal your life’s become a content-delivery system for corporations. So ask yourself: If you can’t sit alone with your thoughts for 10 minutes, who the hell owns you?"
